summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting covering several resignations, mutual aid reimbursements from the 2022 tornado, and the announcement of Homorama 2024 at the Reserves at Stone Pillars Farm. The board approved a contract with MSP Design for engineering and design work on Fire Station 18, established a five-minute community forum policy, and addressed multiple personnel changes including resignations of police and fire department staff.
decisions: Approved payment of bills from January 24 through February 13, 2024
Approved proclamation announcing Homorama 2024 at the Reserves at Stone Pillars Farm (August 24 - September 8, 2024)
Approved revision to leave of absence without pay policy granting township administrator authority to approve absences up to two weeks
Approved increase in appropriations of $351,546.51 in tornado expense fund to reimburse 35 mutual aid partners
Approved increase in appropriations of $8,725.07 for police/fire retention grant with unused funds to be refunded to Ohio
Accepted resignation of Tom Curry from Board of Zoning Appeals effective February 6, 2024
Accepted resignation of Officer Matt Richwine effective February 20, 2024
Accepted resignation of part-time firefighter paramedic David Gray effective January 11, 2024
Accepted resignation of part-time firefighter paramedic Stephen Hill effective January 25, 2024
Accepted resignation of firefighter EMT Grant Rue effective January 13, 2024
Approved updated 2024 road mileage certification to 47.100 miles
Approved community forum policy: five-minute limit per person, at beginning of meeting, on any topic, addressing the board chair
Approved contract with MSP Design for engineering and design of Station 18
Adjourned into executive session at 6:37 PM per ORC 121.22(G)(1)
Approved moving John Langenheim from BZA alternate to BZA full member effective February 13, 2024 with term ending December 31, 2025
Appointed Evan Cordray as volunteer intern effective February 14, 2024 through May 14, 2024
action_items: Administrator Rose to coordinate Homorama 2024 details including Goshen Township booth and Goshen Township day with discounted admission
Post Homorama fact sheet on township website
Chief Meyers to develop bids for construction manager at risk for Station 18 after MSP Design contract is signed
Chief Meyers to speak before Senate Finance Committee on February 27 regarding Station 18 bill
Chief Meyers to put remaining Bureau of Workers' Compensation Grant items (gear dryer, hoods, gloves) into service
Director Meadows to get road mileage certification signed by trustees and submitted
Refund $8,725.07 in unused police/fire retention grant funds to the state of Ohio
Disburse $351,546.51 to 35 mutual aid partners for tornado assistance reimbursement
Trustee Allen offered to help paint/clean at service garage
Include community forum policy announcement at beginning of each year when setting meetings
key_amounts: $351,546.51 - total appropriation increase in tornado expense fund to reimburse mutual aid partners
$263,659.88 - amount provided by Ohio EMA (75% of mutual aid costs)
$87,000 approximately - township's share of mutual aid reimbursement (25%)
$8,725.07 - police/fire retention grant unused funds to be refunded to Ohio
$4,000 - maximum per employee for police/fire retention grant
$48,000 - body camera contract grant secured by Donna Rosick for police department
47.100 miles - updated 2024 road mileage for Goshen Township
notable_topics: Homorama 2024 proclamation at Reserves at Stone Pillars Farm (August 24 - September 8)
Tornado mutual aid reimbursement for 35 agencies from July 6, 2022 tornado
Station 18 engineering/design contract with MSP Design approved
Station 18 bill progressing from House to Senate; Senate Finance Committee testimony scheduled Feb 27
Service garage improvements and cleanup by service department
Multiple personnel resignations (police officer, firefighter/paramedics, BZA member)
Community forum policy established (5-minute limit, any topic, address the chair)
Tragic recent community deaths affecting first responders
Business recovery from 2022 tornado - Wage War reopened
Chat with Chiefs levy event scheduled for next Tuesday at 6:30 PM
Volunteer intern Evan Cordray appointed (high school senior)
John Langenheim promoted from BZA alternate to full member
$48,000 body camera grant secured by part-time grants coordinator Donna Rosick
notes: Executive session held from 6:37 PM to 7:23 PM regarding appointment/employment/discipline/compensation of a public employee. The Amel family was mentioned in the invocation after a fire loss. Trustee Allen referenced a personal fire incident affecting her family, noting Jim was injured but recovering. Rep. Jean Schmidt's involvement in Station 18 legislation was noted. The board discussed the importance of townships allowing open community forums, referencing the Ohio Township Association conference. Homorama is described as the first ever in Goshen Township history. The 2022 tornado aftermath continues to be a significant topic with business reopenings and mutual aid reimbursements still being processed nearly two years later.
title: BOT 2-13-24
